dimensions.description: The agent enters through the repo's routed startup surface before broad reads or edits.
observable_evidence: agentic-workspace start run or correctly selected

dimensions.description: The agent chooses direct, bounded, lane, continuation, takeover, or clarification behavior proportional to task risk.
Durable Memory is consulted, dismissed with reason, or marked not applicable.
Reusable learning is captured or explicitly dismissed instead of left only in chat.
Active planning state is used for continuation and durable handoff when task shape warrants it.
Verification/proof is selected and respected before completion claims.
Final reporting distinguishes full closure, partial closure, blocked closure, and residue ownership.
The agent distinguishes slice-local progress from full parent-intent satisfaction.
observable_evidence: partial closure or full closure claim justified

dimensions.description: Package-owned, host-owned, generated, local-only, and external tracker surfaces are not conflated.
observable_evidence: local config not treated as repo policy

dimensions.description: Stale, invalid, missing, or ambiguous state is diagnosed and repaired or escalated before unsafe work.

failure_taxonomy.description: The agent ignored compact routed context that was visible for the task.
AW did not make the safe next route visible or actionable enough.
CLI output existed but did not give a concrete next action or owner.
Relevant skill guidance was too hidden or prose-heavy to affect behavior.
Durable Memory should have been consulted or dismissed but no decision was observable.
Repeated reusable learning remained only in chat or transient notes.
Active Planning state or durable handoff was needed but absent or ignored.
The agent made a completion claim without adequate proof.
A proof route was selected, but failing, skipped, or partial proof did not constrain the claim.
Residue existed but no owner or follow-up route was recorded.
The agent completed a useful slice but claimed the full parent or lane intent was satisfied without evidence.
The agent confused package, host, generated, local-only, or external-tracker authority.
Local execution guidance should have been routed from structured command/surface facts before running a local command.
Known stale or invalid workspace config blocked startup but no compact recovery packet or next safe action was surfaced.
A documented proof or conformance command drifted from runnable maintained surfaces without maintainer proof failing.
Delegation guidance was surfaced as an action item during bounded direct work where it should have been absent or explicitly non-blocking.
The evaluator cannot observe route, Memory, Planning, proof, residue, or safe-claim decisions required by the scenario.
Artifact-backed host evaluation lacks installed artifact identity, checksum/source, or installed entrypoint proof.
The scenario prompt or fixture cannot distinguish agent failure from evaluator ambiguity.
